name: network-diagram-mcp
description: Network topology editor — Claude builds diagrams via MCP. nmap/CSV import, one-click SSH/Postgres/Redis port-button picker, plus cross-platform URL-handler agent for ssh:// / postgres:// / redis://.
long_description:
Visual network topology editor with 44 MCP tools. Claude / Cursor / Claude Desktop edit a live in-browser diagram at https://map.den.dance/ via a stdio↔WebSocket bridge. Features: create/edit nodes & connections & stickies, multi-sheet workspaces, smart auto-layout (force-directed, cluster-by-type, cluster-by-connection), one-click SSH/Postgres/Redis port buttons on every node card, `nmap -oX` and CSV inventory import with auto-typing, JSON / PNG / PDF export. Local-first, no cloud. Open-source successor to netViz (CA Technologies, 1990–2012).

v1.2 (in-app client picker): when the daemon is running (`npx @den.dance/network-diagram-mcp serve`), clicking an SSH/Postgres/Redis port button in NetMap pops a picker of the installed clients on your machine and launches the one you pick. No silent locked-in default — every click re-detects so newly installed clients show up immediately. The MCP bridge is also now enabled-by-default (opt-out instead of opt-in) so Claude/Cursor can start editing without an extra toggle.

v1.1 (URL-handler agent): `npx @den.dance/network-diagram-mcp install --all` registers ssh:// / postgres:// / redis:// link handlers on Linux (.desktop + xdg-mime), macOS (.app bundle + lsregister) and Windows (HKCU registry). Click any registered URL outside NetMap → spawns the first installed client (psql / redis-cli / iTerm / Windows Terminal / kitty / gnome-terminal / TablePlus / DBeaver / RedisInsight / RDM …). Optional `serve` daemon exposes HTTP /status, /detect, /exec on :47821 for browser-side integration.

Requires an open NetMap browser tab — Settings → ⚙ → Integrations → AI Agent (MCP) is enabled by default. The bundle runs the stdio MCP server locally and bridges to the tab over WebSocket on localhost.

netmap_port.title: WebSocket Port
netmap_port.description: Local WebSocket port the bridge listens on. Must match the URL configured in the NetMap browser tab's Settings → Integrations → AI Agent (MCP).
